danger_signals.visual_form: Animated rats and bats rendered as detailed sprites within castle chambers
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats that can attack Duncan; bites cause damage requiring elixir antidote

danger_signals.visual_form: Floor holes and pit gaps visible in level layouts
danger_signals.meaning: Falls lead to dungeon trap (Trouble 3); vulnerability window when disoriented

danger_signals.visual_form: Duncan circles in place with mumbling animation after wall collision or short fall
danger_signals.meaning: Temporary vulnerability period during which Duncan cannot defend or attack effectively

danger_signals.visual_form: Black Knight figure on throne in final chamber; detailed animated sprite
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate antagonist; requires lever activation to topple; represents evil rule over castle

danger_signals.visual_form: Storm clouds in distance backdrop during opening vista; dark castle silhouette
danger_signals.meaning: Sets tone of danger and foreboding; visual reinforcement of malevolent world-state

safety_signals.visual_form: Small bags containing throwing rocks positioned on ledges and in halls
safety_signals.meaning: Replenishes Duncan's offensive capability; safe to collect during exploration

safety_signals.visual_form: Bottles rendered as distinct collectible items
safety_signals.meaning: One-time antidote to rat/bat bites; provides health protection against common enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: Shield and Fireball marked doors in Great Hall; distinctive door iconography
safety_signals.meaning: Paths leading to essential gear acquisition; progression through optional sectors

safety_signals.visual_form: Ropes and ledges rendered as navigable level geometry
safety_signals.meaning: Movement options that provide escape routes and tactical positioning opportunities

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Great Hall as starting sanctuary; multiple accessible doors represent player agency

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Throne imagery; fist-shaking gesture after toppling
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Single boss sprite with detailed animation; shakes fist in defeat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Throne room as central power location; requires traversal through four castle sections to reach

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Rats and bats as vermin enemies; gargoyle as mythic enforcer
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple animated enemy types distributed across levels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed throughout castle chambers; represent tyrannical control through fauna

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark castle backdrop; storm clouds; detailed enemy presence throughout; Black Knight on throne
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively attack; townspeople terrorized (narrative state); dungeon traps active (Trouble 3)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Hero quest initiated - Duncan challenges tyranny
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Four doors in Great Hall present multiple challenge paths; visual distinction between Fireball, Shield, Trouble, Black Knight routes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Duncan navigates systematically; must collect Fireball and Shield before confronting Black Knight; lever-pulling mechanic enables boss defeat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Black Knight stands and shakes fist (gesture of defeat); gargoyle appears to carry Duncan away
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transition to Trouble 3; visual resolution showing protagonist triumph over antagonist

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Dungeon imprisonment - Consequence for poor navigation
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Trouble 3 chamber rendered as trap dungeon distinct from main castle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Requires escape effort; easier difficulties add delay penalty; harder difficulties force strategic resource gathering

environmental_storytelling.example: Great Hall as central hub with four doors leading to thematic zones (Fireball, Shield, Trouble, Black Knight throne room); door positions and iconography signal difficulty progression and required equipment
Storm clouds backdrop with Bach's Toccata and Fugue in D minor; establishes gothic horror tone and world oppression without dialogue
Rocks in bags and elixir in bottles distributed strategically; players learn to recognize safety through resource proximity
Black Knight's throne room accessible only through central door; vertical position in castle hierarchy; requires prerequisite gear collection
Trouble 3 dungeon exists as consequence space; visual and mechanical distinction signals defeat and confinement under tyranny

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Runs, jumps, ducks; throws rocks with mouse control; navigates ledges and ropes
Circles in place while mumbling incoherently; temporary vulnerability; exposed to enemy attack
Carried away by gargoyle; transported to Trouble 3; visual indication of progression
Attack Duncan; cause damage; can be defeated with thrown rocks or elixir antidote required
Static on throne; represents immobile tyranny; requires lever-pulling mechanic to topple
Stands and shakes fist; gesture of futile defiance; signals end of threat

notes: Dark Castle (1991 Genesis version) uses visual manifestation as primary world-state communicator due to minimal narrative exposition. The game's visual language operates through: (1) color and sprite detail to signal threat hierarchy (enemy types, boss vs. minion); (2) architectural layout (Great Hall hub, four thematic paths) to encode progression and player choice; (3) animated state changes (Duncan's disorientation, Black Knight's defeat gesture) to communicate mechanical vulnerability and narrative resolution; (4) environmental markers (rocks, elixirs, structural hazards) to teach safety/danger distinction through repeated visual exposure. The political situation (tyranny vs. heroic resistance) is conveyed entirely through spatial hierarchy (Black Knight on throne in isolated chamber) and enemy prevalence (minion creatures throughout), with no text exposition required. Difficulty levels alter enemy density and surprise placement, changing visual threat density without changing core manifestation patterns.
